Crypto VC funding is still selective
The cleanest signal is the 2021–2022 peak: quarterly capital reached roughly
$12B
, while deal count went above
1,300
. Since then, the market cooled hard. Q1 2026 is still well below that cycle, even after the 2025 rebound.
• Q1 2026: roughly $4B invested across 355 deals
• Q1 2022 peak: about $12B and 1,300+ deals
• 2025 brought a capital spike, but not a matching deal-count recovery
That’s the kind of funding backdrop that makes marketing budgets more defensive: less slack, more scrutiny, and more pressure to prove conversion. It fits the shift toward proof-driven distribution.
Data source:
Galaxy Research
Related read:
Web3 Marketing in 2027: Adapting to bear market budgets

The Beginner’s Guide to Physical AI Stack
Physical AI encompasses industrial robots, warehouse automation, and autonomous vehicles
The 5 layer stack: Brain (AI/VLA models), Body (humanoid), Parts (actuators, gearboxes, sensors), Fuel (training data via teleoperation, simulation, and fleet experience), and Proving Grounds (actual deployments)
https://x.com/muskan_kalra24/status/2077090406819647871?s=20

Why SK Hynix ADR trades at a large premium on Nasdaq but can't be arbed
• ADR (American Depositary Receipt) = certificate issued by a US bank that represents shares of a foreign company
• SK Hynix has 2 versions of its stock: one on Korea market and the ADR version recently listed on Nasdaq
• Each ADR is 1/10 of the local share and currently trading at a large premium
• Premium exist because US/global investors strongly prefer the ADR + easier custody/settlement + local Korean shares harder to access
• Conversion of the stock is 1-way
• ADR → Local shares: Easy and free to convert
• Local shares → ADR: Requires company consent / is heavily restricted
• Similar like the Kimchi Premium for Bitcoin that traded much higher on Korean exchanges
https://x.com/ZixiStablestock/status/2077060055392817466?s=20

tweet.text: Same SK hynix, but its US ADR trades up to 37% above the Seoul shares — and no one can arb it. ADRs convert into local shares, but local shares can't convert back without company consent. Only new ADR issuance closes the gap. TSMC's ADR premium has held ~19% for years.

CXMT (ChangXin Memory Technologies) listed on Hyperliquid pre-IPO perps
• China's SK Hynix, China’s largest and the world’s fourth-largest DRAM manufacture (~ 7-9% global DRAM market share)
• Targeted for a July 27th IPO on the Shanghai Stock Exchange STAR Market
• One of Asia’s largest IPOs of 2026 and China’s biggest semiconductor listing in years
• The company received heavy state backing in the form of capital, land, policy support, and alignment with national semiconductor self-reliance goals (part of broader “Made in China 2025” and dual-circulation strategies)
• IPO proceeds are earmarked for further fab expansion, capacity ramps through 2026-2028, and technology upgrades aimed at AI server applications.
SOURCE

Nobody is close to breaking ECC
Although theoretical advances have dramatically reduced the quantum hardware needed to break Elliptic Curve Cryptography, current quantum computers are still orders of magnitude away from having enough error-corrected qubits to execute the attack
Consequently, standard internet protocols and cryptocurrencies face no immediate risk, though exposed public addresses will eventually need to transition to quantum-resistant encryption
https://derrickcui.substack.com/p/nobody-is-close-to-breaking-ecc

Latent Space as a New Medium
AI’s massive compression of human knowledge creates high-dimensional "latent spaces" that map concepts as directional vectors
These latent spaces will serve as a new creative medium, enabling scientists and artists to prospect gaps between ideas, simulate realities, and uncover hidden meta-patterns
https://kevinkelly.substack.com/p/latent-space-as-a-new-medium

tweet.text:
Wix at 1x ARR is not a SaaS bloodbath. 

It is a reckoning for businesses that were always just sales and marketing operations dressed up as software.

1. The client-server to SaaS shift was more disruptive than anything AI is doing to software right now. This is incremental. The market is finally pricing it that way.

2. SaaS was never that hard to spin up. When the moat is a sales team and a logo, the multiple compresses. That is not a surprise, that is gravity.

3. The companies that survive this are the ones with complex customer relationships and data sets that cannot be recreated. Wix does not have that. Base44 might not either.

4. Vibe coding tools doing $170M ARR getting priced at $2.1BN is the same mistake in reverse. Getting real workflow change adopted at scale takes longer than the ARR curve suggests.

5. The best software companies right now are not pure software companies. The ones with physical world integration and proprietary data loops are harder to replicate and har

Galaxy Research: Inference Capital Markets
> A new onchain financial layer is emerging around AI inference, using crypto to tokenize compute access, finance GPU infrastructure, and create markets for AI services.
> For now, inference capital markets, both onchain and off, are tiny relative to the growth of the AI industry. For onchain offerings to scale, they have to prove the advantage they bring is sustainable.
Source:
https://www.galaxy.com/insights/research/inference-capital-markets-ai-compute-gpu-futures-onchain-crypto
